channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Disruption with Copilot Coding Agent Sessions
item.description: Mar 20, 01:58 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available.Mar 20, 01:26 UTCUpdate - We are rolling out our mitigation and are seeing recovery.Mar 20,...

item.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
item.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets --...

item.title: Speagle Malware Hijacks Cobra DocGuard to Steal Data via Compromised Servers
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new malware dubbed Speagle that hijacks the functionality and infrastructure of a legitimate program called Cobra DocGuard. "Speagle is designed to surreptitiously harvest sensitive information from infected computers and transmit it to a Cobra DocGuard...

item.title: 54 EDR Killers Use BYOVD to Exploit 34 Signed Vulnerable Drivers and Disable Security
item.description: A new analysis of endpoint detection and response (EDR) killers has revealed that 54 of them leverage a technique known as bring your own vulnerable driver (BYOVD) by abusing a total of 34 vulnerable drivers. EDR killer programs have been a common presence in ransomware intrusions as they offer a...

item.title: Oasis Security Raises $120 Million for Agentic Access Management
item.description: The company will invest in R&D, product expansion across AI frameworks, and in scaling go-to-market and sales efforts. The post Oasis Security Raises $120 Million for Agentic Access Management appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: 1stProtect Emerges From Stealth With $20 Million in Funding
item.description: The company’s endpoint security platform monitors behavior and verifies user intent to stop cyberattacks in real time. The post 1stProtect Emerges From Stealth With $20 Million in Funding appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Critical ScreenConnect Vulnerability Exposes Machine Keys
item.description: Latest ScreenConnect version adds encrypted storage and management to prevent unauthorized access to machine keys. The post Critical ScreenConnect Vulnerability Exposes Machine Keys appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Privacy Platform Cloaked Raises $375M to Expand Enterprise Reach
item.description: Cloaked plans to introduce AI agents designed to act on behalf of users to monitor, manage, and enforce privacy preferences and security postures. The post Privacy Platform Cloaked Raises $375M to Expand Enterprise Reach appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Git operations for users in the west coast are experiencing an increase in latency
item.description: Mar 19, 16:25 UTCInvestigating - We are investigating reports of degraded performance for Git Operations

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.6
item.description: Mar 19, 16:16 UTCResolved - This incident started at 8:53 PT / 15:53 UTC and the errors have now returned to baseline since 9:14 PT / 16:14 UTC.Mar 19, 15:59 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Cloudflare Pages API is seeing increased error rate.
item.description: Mar 19, 15:57 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 19, 15:54 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare Pages API is seeing increased error rate. This affects the Workers & Pages section in the Cloudflare Dashboard, as well as Workers Builds. Deployed Workers and Pages projects are unaffected

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Sonnet 4.6
item.description: Mar 19, 15:46 UTCResolved - As of 8:42 PT / 15:42 UTC the error rate has now returned to baseline.Mar 19, 15:37 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating elevated errors that started at 8:23 PT / 15:23 UTC.

item.title: Iran Readied Cyberattack Capabilities for Response Prior to Epic Fury
item.description: Analysis reveals a six-month buildup of Iran-linked cyber infrastructure, including US-based shell companies, designed to weather kinetic strikes and ensure the resilience of its global hacking operations. The post Iran Readied Cyberattack Capabilities for Response Prior to Epic Fury appeared first...

item.title: Issues with Copilot Coding Agent
item.description: Mar 19, 14:32 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available.Mar 19, 14:06 UTCUpdate - Copilot is operating normally.Mar 19, 14:02 UTCUpdate - We are...

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: FortiGate RaaS, Citrix Exploits, MCP Abuse, LiveChat Phish & More
item.description: ThreatsDay Bulletin is back on The Hacker News, and this week feels off in a familiar way. Nothing loud, nothing breaking everything at once. Just a lot of small things that shouldn’t work anymore but still do. Some of it looks simple, almost sloppy, until you see how well it lands. Other...

item.title: **Introducing SPEED-Bench: A Unified and Diverse Benchmark for Speculative Decoding**
New Perseus Android Banking Malware Monitors Notes Apps to Extract Sensitive Data
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a new Android malware family called Perseus that's being actively distributed in the wild with an aim to conduct device takeover (DTO) and financial fraud. Perseus is built upon the foundations of Cerberus and Phoenix, at the same time evolving into a "more...

item.title: The Download: Quantum computing for health, and why the world doesn’t recycle more nuclear waste
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. A $5 million prize awaits proof that quantum computers can solve health care problems  In a laboratory on the outskirts of Oxford, a quantum...

item.title: How to secure your online meetings
item.description: Advice for small and medium-sized organisations on choosing, setting up and using online meeting services safely.

item.title: Increased HTTP 530 Errors in Atlanta (ATL)
item.description: Mar 19, 11:36 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating an increased rate of HTTP 530 errors specifically affecting traffic routed through our Atlanta (ATL) data center. We are working to identify the root cause and will provide updates as they become available.

item.title: How Ceros Gives Security Teams Visibility and Control in Claude Code
item.description: Security teams have spent years building identity and access controls for human users and service accounts. But a new category of actor has quietly entered most enterprise environments, and it operates entirely outside those controls. Claude Code, Anthropic's AI coding agent, is now running across...

item.title: A $5 million prize awaits proof that quantum computers can solve health care problems
item.description: I’m standing in front of a quantum computer built out of atoms and light at the UK’s National Quantum Computing Centre on the outskirts of Oxford. On a laboratory table, a complex matrix of mirrors and lenses surrounds a Rubik’s Cube–size cell where 100 cesium atoms are suspended in grid formation...

item.title: Why the world doesn’t recycle more nuclear waste
item.description: The prospect of making trash useful is always fascinating to me. Whether it’s used batteries, solar panels, or spent nuclear fuel, getting use out of something destined for disposal sounds like a win all around. In nuclear energy, figuring out what to do with waste has always been a challenge,...

item.title: Network Performance Issues in Mumbai, India
item.description: Mar 19, 08:43 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Mar 19, 08:37 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating network performance issues that may impact a subset of users in Mumbai, India. Some users may experience increased latency or intermittent errors....

item.title: Xiaomi Unveils Three In-House Foundation Models, Confirms MiMo-V2-Pro Identity
item.description: Xiaomi has launched three foundation models, including the trillion‑parameter MiMo‑V2‑Pro (previously known as Hunter Alpha), which ranked 8th globally on Artificial Analysis.

item.title: Pop Mart, Sony Pictures to Develop Live-Action/CG Film Based on LABUBU IP
item.description: Pop Mart and Sony Pictures are developing a live‑action/CGI film based on the popular LABUBU character from THE MONSTERS series, directed by Paul King.

item.title: ByteDance Launches AI Short-Drama Agent Powered by Seedance 2.0
item.description: ByteDance's XiaoYunQue AI platform has launched the industry's first short drama Agent powered by Seedance 2.0, enabling users to generate complete video episodes from scripts of up to 100,000 words with consistent character design and scene styles .

item.title: Sigenergy Files for Hong Kong IPO for Third Time After Previous Lapses
item.description: Sigenergy, a Shanghai-based energy storage company founded by former Huawei executive Xu Yingtong, has filed for a Hong Kong IPO after achieving revenue of RMB 5.64 billion in the first nine months of 2025 with net profit reaching RMB 1.89 billion .

item.title: Kunlun Tech’s SkyReels-V4 Tops Global Text-to-Video Ranking
item.description: Kunlun Tech's SkyReels-V4 model has ranked first globally in the "Text to Video (With Audio)" category on the Artificial Analysis leaderboard, surpassing mainstream models including Kling 3.0, Google Veo 3.1, and OpenAI Sora 2 .

item.title: [AINews] MiniMax 2.7: GLM-5 at 1/3 cost SOTA Open Model
item.description: congrats MiniMax!!

item.title: A Dynamic Survey of Fuzzy, Intuitionistic Fuzzy, Neutrosophic, Plithogenic, and Extensional Sets
item.description: arXiv:2603.15667v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Real-world phenomena often exhibit vagueness, partial truth, and incomplete information. To model such uncertainty in a mathematically rigorous way, many generalized set-theoretic frameworks have been introduced, including Fuzzy Sets [1],...

item.title: Quantum-Secure-By-Construction (QSC): A Paradigm Shift For Post-Quantum Agentic Intelligence
item.description: arXiv:2603.15668v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As agentic artificial intelligence systems scale across globally distributed and long lived infrastructures, secure and policy compliant communication becomes a fundamental systems challenge. This challenge grows more serious in the quantum era, where...

item.title: I Know What I Don't Know: Latent Posterior Factor Models for Multi-Evidence Probabilistic Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2603.15670v2 Announce Type: new Abstract: Real-world decision-making, from tax compliance assessment to medical diagnosis, requires aggregating multiple noisy and potentially contradictory evidence sources. Existing approaches either lack explicit uncertainty quantification (neural...

item.title: Theoretical Foundations of Latent Posterior Factors: Formal Guarantees for Multi-Evidence Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2603.15674v2 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present a complete theoretical characterization of Latent Posterior Factors (LPF), a principled framework for aggregating multiple heterogeneous evidence items in probabilistic prediction tasks. Multi-evidence reasoning arises pervasively in...

item.title: Survey of Various Fuzzy and Uncertain Decision-Making Methods
item.description: arXiv:2603.15709v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Decision-making in real applications is often affected by vagueness, incomplete information, heterogeneous data, and conflicting expert opinions. This survey reviews uncertainty-aware multi-criteria decision-making (MCDM) and organizes the field into...

item.title: A foundation model for electrodermal activity data
item.description: arXiv:2603.16878v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Foundation models have recently extended beyond natural language and vision to timeseries domains, including physiological signals. However, progress in electrodermal activity (EDA) modeling is hindered by the absence of large-scale, curated, and...

item.title: Federated Multi Agent Deep Learning and Neural Networks for Advanced Distributed Sensing in Wireless Networks
item.description: arXiv:2603.16881v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-agent deep learning (MADL), including multi-agent deep reinforcement learning (MADRL), distributed/federated training, and graph-structured neural networks, is becoming a unifying framework for decision-making and inference in wireless systems...

item.title: Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Dynamic Pricing: Balancing Profitability,Stability and Fairness
item.description: arXiv:2603.16888v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Dynamic pricing in competitive retail markets requires strategies that adapt to fluctuating demand and competitor behavior. In this work, we present a systematic empirical evaluation of multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) approaches-specifically...

item.title: From Language to Action in Arabic: Reliable Structured Tool Calling via Data-Centric Fine-Tuning
item.description: arXiv:2603.16901v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Function-calling language models are essential for agentic AI systems that translate natural language into executable structured actions, yet existing models exhibit severe structural instability when applied to Arabic. We present...

item.title: What on Earth is AlphaEarth? Hierarchical structure and functional interpretability for global land cover
item.description: arXiv:2603.16911v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Geospatial foundation models generate high-dimensional embeddings that achieve strong predictive performance, yet their internal organization remains obscure, limiting their scientific use. Recent interpretability studies relate Google AlphaEarth...

item.title: MoLoRA: Composable Specialization via Per-Token Adapter Routing
item.description: arXiv:2603.15965v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-adapter serving systems route entire sequences to a single adapter, forcing a choice when requests span multiple domains. This assumption fails in two important settings: (1) multimodal generation, where text and image tokens require different...

item.title: Robust Language Identification for Romansh Varieties
item.description: arXiv:2603.15969v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The Romansh language has several regional varieties, called idioms, which sometimes have limited mutual intelligibility. Despite this linguistic diversity, there has been a lack of documented efforts to build a language identification (LID) system...

item.title: Aligning Paralinguistic Understanding and Generation in Speech LLMs via Multi-Task Reinforcement Learning
item.description: arXiv:2603.15981v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Speech large language models (LLMs) observe paralinguistic cues such as prosody, emotion, and non-verbal sounds--crucial for intent understanding. However, leveraging these cues faces challenges: limited training data, annotation difficulty, and...

item.title: NLP Occupational Emergence Analysis: How Occupations Form and Evolve in Real Time -- A Zero-Assumption Method Demonstrated on AI in the US Technology Workforce, 2022-2026
item.description: arXiv:2603.15998v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Occupations form and evolve faster than classification systems can track. We propose that a genuine occupation is a self-reinforcing structure (a bipartite co-attractor) in which a shared professional vocabulary makes practitioners cohesive as a...

item.title: RadAnnotate: Large Language Models for Efficient and Reliable Radiology Report Annotation
item.description: arXiv:2603.16002v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Radiology report annotation is essential for clinical NLP, yet manual labeling is slow and costly. We present RadAnnotate, an LLM-based framework that studies retrieval-augmented synthetic reports and confidence-based selective automation to reduce...

item.title: Disruption with Copilot Coding Agent sessions
item.description: Mar 19, 02:25 UTCUpdate - We are seeing widespread issues starting and viewing Copilot Agent sessions. We have a hypothesis for the cause and are working on remediation.Mar 19, 02:05 UTCInvestigating - We are investigating reports of impacted performance for some GitHub services.

item.title: Disruption with some GitHub services
item.description: Mar 19, 01:44 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available.Mar 19, 01:43 UTCUpdate - We are seeing recovery in git operations for customers on the...

item.title: Elevated errors across surfaces
item.description: Mar 19, 01:38 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 19, 01:21 UTCMonitoring - From 23:59–00:30 UTC, users experienced elevated errors when trying to authenticate across various Claude surfaces including Claude.ai and Claude Code. This issue has been resolved, and we are monitoring...

item.title: Performance degradation in one of the Cloudflare Los Angeles POP
item.description: Mar 18, 23:48 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 18, 21:29 UTCMonitoring - We have mitigated an issue in one of the Cloudflare Los Angeles POPs causing performance degradation between 20:32 to 20:53 UTC

item.title: Dashboard Login Issues
item.description: Mar 18, 20:30 UTCInvestigating - Investigating issues with logging into the Dashboard silently failing, especially via SSO
